title: Docker
description: Containerization. Images, containers, Dockerfile, Compose, volumes, networking, and orchestration.

items.label: Install & Verify
items.code:
# Install on Ubuntu
sudo apt update
sudo apt install docker.io -y
sudo systemctl enable --now docker

# Verify
sudo docker run hello-world
docker --version
docker compose version

# Post-install (run without sudo)
sudo usermod -aG docker $USER
# Logout and log back in

# Basic commands
docker ps                    # running containers
docker ps -a                 # all containers
docker images                # list images
docker info                  # system info

items.label: Dockerfile
items.code:
# Dockerfile - build instructions

FROM node:20-alpine          # base image

WORKDIR /app                 # working directory

COPY package*.json ./        # copy dependency files
RUN npm ci --only=production # install dependencies

COPY . .                     # copy source

EXPOSE 3000                  # document port

CMD ["node", "server.js"]    # default command

# Build the image
docker build -t myapp:latest .
docker build -t myapp:1.0.0 -f Dockerfile.prod .

items.label: Run & Manage
items.code:
# Run
 docker run myapp                  # foreground
docker run -d myapp                # detached (background)
docker run -p 3000:3000 myapp      # port mapping (host:container)
docker run --name myapp myapp      # named container
docker run -e NODE_ENV=production myapp   # env variable
docker run -v $(pwd):/app myapp    # volume mount
docker run --rm myapp              # auto-remove on stop

# Manage
docker ps                          # running
docker ps -a                       # all
docker start myapp                 # start stopped
docker stop myapp                  # graceful stop
docker kill myapp                  # force stop
docker restart myapp               # restart

# Logs & exec
docker logs myapp                  # view logs
docker logs -f myapp               # follow logs
docker logs --tail 50 myapp        # last 50 lines
docker exec -it myapp sh           # enter container
docker exec -it myapp bash         # if bash available

# Inspect
docker inspect myapp               # detailed info
docker stats                       # live resource usage
docker top myapp                   # running processes

items.label: Image Management
items.code:
# List & remove
docker images                    # all images
docker rmi myapp:latest          # remove image
docker image prune               # remove dangling images
docker image prune -a            # remove all unused

# Tag & push
docker tag myapp username/myapp:latest
docker login                     # login to registry
docker push username/myapp:latest

# Pull
docker pull node:20-alpine
docker pull username/myapp:latest

# Save & load (air-gapped)
docker save -o myapp.tar myapp
docker load -i myapp.tar

# History & layers
docker history myapp:latest      # layer history
docker image inspect myapp

# Common base images
node:20-alpine       # small Node.js (117MB)
python:3.12-slim     # Python slim (147MB)
nginx:alpine         # Nginx web server (24MB)
ubuntu:22.04         # Ubuntu (77MB)
alpine:latest        # Alpine Linux (7MB)

items.label: Persistent Data
items.code:
# Named volume
docker volume create mydata
docker run -v mydata:/data myapp

# Bind mount
docker run -v $(pwd):/app myapp           # Linux/macOS
docker run -v "%cd%":/app myapp          # Windows CMD

# Read-only bind mount
docker run -v $(pwd):/app:ro myapp

# tmpfs mount (in-memory, for secrets)
docker run --tmpfs /tmp myapp

# Manage volumes
docker volume ls
docker volume inspect mydata
docker volume prune

# Copy files between host and container
docker cp file.txt myapp:/app/file.txt
docker cp myapp:/app/output.txt .

# Backup volume
docker run --rm -v mydata:/source -v $(pwd):/backup alpine \
  tar czf /backup/mydata.tar.gz -C /source .

items.label: Docker Networks
items.code:
# Network types
docker network ls
# bridge: default, isolated network
# host: shares host network
# none: no network

# Create network
docker network create mynetwork

# Connect container on startup
docker run --network mynetwork --name web myapp

# Connect existing container
docker network connect mynetwork myapp

# DNS resolution
# Containers on same network resolve by name
# ping web (not IP, just container name)

# Port publishing
docker run -p 80:3000 myapp    # host:container
docker run -p 127.0.0.1:8080:3000 myapp  # bind to specific IP

# Inspect network
docker network inspect mynetwork
docker inspect --format='{{range .NetworkSettings.Networks}}{{.IPAddress}}{{end}}' myapp

items.label: Compose File
items.code:
# docker-compose.yml
version: '3.8'

services:
  web:
    build: .
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
      - DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:pass@db:5432/myapp
    depends_on:
      - db
    volumes:
      - .:/app
      - /app/node_modules

  db:
    image: postgres:15
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_DB=myapp
      - POSTGRES_USER=user
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=secret
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"

volumes:
  pgdata:

items.label: Compose Commands
items.code:
docker compose up           # start services
docker compose up -d        # start in background
docker compose down         # stop and remove
docker compose down -v      # stop + remove volumes
docker compose ps           # list services
docker compose logs -f      # follow all logs
docker compose logs web -f  # follow specific service
docker compose exec web sh  # shell into service
docker compose build        # rebuild images
docker compose pull         # pull latest images
docker compose restart      # restart all
docker compose config       # validate compose file

# Profiles
docker compose --profile dev up
docker compose --profile prod up

items.label: Optimization Tips
items.code:
# 1. Use multi-stage builds
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM nginx:alpine
COPY --from=builder /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html

# 2. Minimize layers
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    package1 \
    package2 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# 3. Order matters (cache layers)
COPY package*.json ./   # changes rarely
RUN npm install         # cached if package.json same
COPY . .                # changes often

# 4. Use .dockerignore
node_modules/
npm-debug.log
.env
.git
*.md

# 5. Run as non-root
RUN addgroup -S appgroup && adduser -S appuser -G appgroup
USER appuser

# 6. Healthcheck
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s \
  CMD curl -f http://localhost/ || exit 1
